criteria.label: Define supervised learning and name its four core elements (x, y, loss, model).
criteria.example: Write one sentence: supervised learning learns f(x)≈y from labeled (x,y) pairs by minimizing L(ŷ,y).

criteria.label: Distinguish supervised learning from unsupervised learning using data requirements.
criteria.example: Given a dataset of images with no tags, explain why clustering is unsupervised and what labels would be needed to make it supervised.

criteria.label: Identify regression vs classification as supervised sub-types from a problem description.
criteria.example: Classify three tasks (stock price, digit recognition, customer segment discovery) as supervised regression, supervised classification, or not supervised.

criteria.label: Implement a minimal supervised train/predict workflow on a toy dataset.
criteria.example: Split (X,y) into train/test, fit LogisticRegression on train, report accuracy on test with aligned row order preserved.

criteria.label: Explain the supervised training loop and where labels are vs are not needed.
criteria.example: Draw or list steps: labeled train → loss → update θ; inference uses x only. Note where validation labels are still required.
